overview: The Gemara discusses the laws of acquisition when one rides an animal and another leads it, analyzing the status of the rider (רכוב) versus the leader (מנהיג) and the concept of 'מוסירה'.

sugyaBoundaries.gist: The Gemara discusses the laws of acquisition when one rides an animal and another leads it, analyzing the status of the rider (רכוב) versus the leader (מנהיג) and the concept of 'מוסירה'.

steps.title: Resolution: The בְּרֵיתָא teaches a new case
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by stating that the Baraita is teaching a new case where the rider is not leading.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the challenge by reinterpreting the בְּרֵיתָא. It explains the בְּרֵיתָא addresses a rider who is not leading.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The בְּרֵיתָא excludes a passive rider. A rider who does not lead does not acquire. This aligns with previous conclusions without contradiction.

phrases.english: This is the law that even a rider,
And the case that it teaches: one who pulls and leads –
To exclude from Rabbi Yehuda who said:

phrases.aramaic: עַד שֶׁתְּהֵא מְשִׁיכָה בְּגָמָל וְהַנְהָגָה בַּחֲמוֹר,
phrases.english: Until it be pulling on a camel and leading on a donkey,

phrases.aramaic: קָמַשְׁמַע לַן דַּאֲפִילּוּ אִיפְּכָא נָמֵי קָנֵי.
phrases.english: It came to teach us that even the opposite one also acquires.

steps.triggerLanguage: הוא הדין דאפילו רכוב, והא דקתני מושך ומנהיג

steps.title: Challenge to the מִשְׁנָה's wording
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges why the Mishnah lists two separate cases of two people pulling/leading a camel and a donkey, suggesting they should be combined into one statement.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא attacks the redundancy of the מִשְׁנָה's phrasing. It questions why the מִשְׁנָה lists camel and donkey separately instead of combining them.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גמרא argues a single phrase should suffice. The distinction between camel and donkey seems unnecessary for the legal principle. This sets up a need for explanation.

keyTerms.meaning: Whether in a camel or in a donkey

phrases.english: If so, let us combine them and let us teach them:

phrases.aramaic: שְׁנַיִם שֶׁהָיוּ מוֹשְׁכִין וּמַנְהִיגִין בֵּין בְּגָמָל בֵּין בַּחֲמוֹר!
phrases.english: Two who were pulling and leading, either on a camel or on a donkey!

rashi.hebrew: אי הכי - דלתנא קמא איפכא קני אמאי קתני משיכה בגמל והנהגה בחמור ליערבינהו וכו' מושכין ומנהיגין:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's challenge assumes the תנא קמא holds that קנין is acquired through משיכה in a camel and הנהגה in a donkey, so the Mishnah should have combined these into a single phrase of 'מושכין ומנהיגין'.

tosafot.hebrew: אי הכי ליערבינהו ולתנינהו - לפי מאי דס"ד דרכוב קני ומשיכה והנהגה בין בגמל בין בחמור במדה זו קנה אתא למעוטי שום קנין אחר או רכוב במקום מנהיג:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות clarifies that the Gemara's challenge arises from the assumption that רכוב acquires קנין and that משיכה and הנהגה apply to both a camel and a donkey, meaning the Mishnah's phrasing is intended to exclude other forms of קנין or to specify רכוב in place of הנהגה.

steps.title: Resolution: Disagreement on acquisition methods
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the redundancy by explaining that there is a disagreement among sages about whether pulling (משכה) acquires a donkey and whether leading (הנהגה) acquires a camel.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the redundancy challenge by citing a halachic disagreement. It explains the מִשְׁנָה lists both cases to cover a dispute.
steps.deeperAnalysis: Some hold pulling acquires a donkey, others hold leading acquires a camel. Both cases are necessary to address the disagreement. This justifies the מִשְׁנָה's precise wording.

keyTerms.meaning: There is one aspect where he does not acquire

phrases.english: There is one side that does not acquire:
There are those who say: dragging a donkey,
And there are those who say: leading a camel.

rashi.hebrew: איכא חד צד דלא קני - להכי לא מצי למתני בתרוייהו משיכה והנהגה ותנא הכי מושכין בגמל ומנהיגין בחמור או שהיה אחד מושך ואחד מנהיג באחד מהן אותו שדרכו בשניהם:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Mishnah could not combine משיכה and הנהגה into a single clause because there is a dispute regarding which method acquires which animal, so the Mishnah lists them separately to cover the case where one person uses the method valid for both animals according to the dispute.

steps.title: Challenge from the second clause
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution by citing the second clause of the Mishnah, which states 'by this measure he acquired', implying that riding does not acquire.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a new challenge from the second clause of the מִשְׁנָה. It asks what 'by this measure he acquired' excludes.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The phrase seems to exclude riding as a method of acquisition. This contradicts the previous resolution that riding might acquire. The גמרא must reconsider the status of riding.

phrases.aramaic: וְאִית דְּמוֹתֵיב מִסֵּיפָא: בְּמִדָּה זוֹ קָנָה.
phrases.english: And there are those who say from the latter clause: By this measure he acquired.
"By this measure" — to exclude what?
Is it not to exclude a rider?!
No, to exclude a saddle.

rashi.hebrew: במדה הזאת - קס"ד במשיכה או בהנהגה בין בגמל בין בחמור:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the Gemara's assumption is that the Mishnah's phrase 'by this measure' refers to either משיכה or הנהגה, whether the animal is a camel or a donkey.

steps.title: Challenge: Equating to רַבִּי יְהוּדָה
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution by arguing that if the exclusion is of the opposite case, then the Mishnah is identical to Rabbi Yehuda's opinion.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a challenge based on the previous resolution. It argues the מִשְׁנָה becomes identical to רַבִּי יְהוּדָה's opinion.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If the exclusion targets the opposite case, the מִשְׁנָה is redundant. This forces the גמרא to find a new resolution. The previous explanation is insufficient.

keyTerms.meaning: One aspect where he did not acquire

phrases.english: If so, this is Rabbi Yehuda!

phrases.aramaic: אִיכָּא בֵּינַיְיהוּ צַד אֶחָד דְּלֹא קָנָה:
phrases.english: There is between them one aspect that he did not acquire:
Some say: dragging a donkey,
And some say: leading a camel.

tosafot.hebrew: אי הכי היינו ר' יהודה - הוה מצי לאקשויי מגופה דקתני או שהיה אחד מושך ואחד מנהיג ש"מ דבחד צד קני:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that the Gemara could have derived the principle that acquisition occurs in only one direction directly from the Mishnah's own wording of 'one drags and one guides', rather than needing to challenge it against רבי יהודה.

steps.title: Proof from a בְּרֵיתָא
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara cites a baraita that states a rider on a donkey and one holding the reins both acquire, suggesting that riding does acquire.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא brings a proof from a בְּרֵיתָא to support riding as acquisition. It cites a case where a rider on a donkey acquires.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The בְּרֵיתָא states a rider and one holding reins both acquire. This suggests riding is a valid method. It contradicts the exclusion implied earlier.

phrases.english: Come and hear:

phrases.aramaic: אֶחָד רָכוּב חֲמוֹר וְאֶחָד תָּפוּס בְּמוֹסֵירָה –
phrases.english: One riding a donkey and one holding the reins –
This one acquires the donkey,
and this one acquires the reins.
Learn from it: A rider acquires!

rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the person holding the reins (תפוס במוסירה) is not actively leading the donkey, distinguishing this case from one where the reins-holder is also the leader.

rashi.hebrew: ש"מ רכוב קני - שלא במקום מנהיג וכיון דשלא במקום מנהיג קני כוליה במקום מנהיג פלגי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the baraita proves riding acquires only when the rider is not the one leading the donkey, and since acquisition occurs in that non-leading scenario, the case where the rider is also the leader must be divided between the two parties.

steps.title: Resolution: Leading with Feet
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by explaining that the rider is also leading the donkey with his feet.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the challenge by clarifying the rider's action. It is a setup line explaining the rider's dual role.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The rider is not merely sitting; he is actively guiding the animal with his feet. This distinction preserves the principle that riding alone does not acquire, but leading does.

phrases.english: Here too, by leading with his feet.

phrases.aramaic: אִי הָכִי, נִקְנֵי נָמֵי רָכוּב בְּמוֹסֵירָה!
phrases.english: If so, let us also acquire riding on reins!

phrases.aramaic: אֵימָא: זֶה קָנָה חֲמוֹר וַחֲצִי מוֹסֵירָה,
phrases.english: Say: This one acquired a donkey and half the reins,
and this one acquired half the reins.

rashi.hebrew: נקני נמי רכוב במוסירה - שבראש החמור שהוא תכשיט החמור וכחמור דמי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the challenge assumes the rider acquires the donkey because the saddle (מוסירה) is considered an ornament of the donkey and thus equivalent to the donkey itself.

tosafot.hebrew: ונקני נמי רכוב במוסירה - אי גרסינן אי הכי ה"פ אי אמרת בשלמא כשאין מנהיג ברגליו ורכוב קני הבהמה משום דתפיס בה א"ש דלא קני מוסירה דבמטלטלין לא שייך קנין של מוסירה אלא בהגבהה או משיכה אבל אי מיירי במנהיג ברגליו לקני נמי מוסירה במשיכת הבהמה אי אמרינן משוך בהמה זו וקני כלים שעליה קני דלקמן מיבעיא לן אי קני אי לא קני ליפשוט מהכא דלא מהני:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that if the Gemara's challenge assumes riding alone acquires the animal, then the rider should also acquire the saddle via the animal's acquisition, but since the Gemara rejects this, it implies that leading with feet is necessary for acquisition, and thus the saddle is not acquired unless the animal itself is acquired through leading.

steps.title: Question: How Does the Leader Acquire?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks how the leader can acquire the reins if he is only holding them.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a question about the leader's acquisition method. It is a transition line questioning the mechanics of the previous resolution.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The text asks how the leader acquires the reins if he is only holding them. This forces a re-evaluation of the acquisition mechanism.

keyTerms.meaning: In what way does he acquire

phrases.aramaic: בִּשְׁלָמָא רָכוּב קָנֵי, דְּקָמַגְבַּהּ לֵיהּ בֶּן דַּעַת.
phrases.english: Certainly, the rider acquires, for a person of understanding lifts it for him.
But the one holding the reins — in what does he acquire?

steps.triggerLanguage: בִּשְׁלָמָא רָכוּב קָנֵי, דְּקָמַגְבַּהּ לֵיהּ בֶּן דַּעַת. אֶלָּא תָּפוּס בְּמוֹסֵירָה בְּמַאי קָנֵי?

rashi.hebrew: בשלמא רכוב - מצית למימר דקנה בהגבהת חבירו שהגביה ראשו השני מן הקרקע דקא מגבה לה בן דעת:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that when the leader is riding, he acquires the reins through the הֲגָבָה of the other person, since the other person lifts the second head of the animal off the ground, and a בֶּן דַּעַת can acquire for another.

rashi.hebrew: אלא תפוס במוסירה במאי קני - פלגא והלא ראשה השני מונח במקומו והגבהת הפקר לא קניא עד דעקר לכוליה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that when the leader is merely holding the reins, he cannot acquire them because only half of the animal is lifted, and הֲגָבָה of a הֲפָקָר does not effect acquisition unless the entire item is uprooted.

steps.title: Resolution: Partial Acquisition
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the question by suggesting that the rider acquires the donkey and almost all the reins, while the leader acquires only the part he holds.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the question by splitting the acquisition. It is a setup line detailing the division of ownership.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The rider acquires the donkey and most of the reins. The leader acquires only the specific portion he holds in his hand.

keyTerms.meaning: What he holds in his hand

phrases.aramaic: אֵימָא: זֶה קָנָה חֲמוֹר וְכוּלֵּיהּ מוֹסֵירָה,
phrases.english: Say: This one acquired a donkey and all of it is a loss,
and this one acquired what fits in his hand.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֵימָא: זֶה קָנָה חֲמוֹר וְכוּלֵּיהּ מוֹסֵירָה, וְזֶה קָנָה מַה שֶּׁתָּפוּס בְּיָדוֹ.

steps.title: Challenge: Acquisition on Behalf of Another
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution by arguing that one cannot acquire on behalf of another if they are not acting with the intention to do so.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא challenges the previous resolution using a principle of agency. It is a setup line introducing a new difficulty.
steps.deeperAnalysis: One cannot acquire an object for another person without that person's explicit intention. This contradicts the idea that the rider acquires the reins for the leader.

keyTerms.meaning: One who lifts a found object for his friend
With the intention of his friend

phrases.english: What is this?

phrases.aramaic: אִם תִּימְצֵי לוֹמַר הַמַּגְבִּיהַּ מְצִיאָה לַחֲבֵירוֹ
phrases.english: If you say that the one who lifts a found item for his friend
his friend acquires

phrases.aramaic: הָנֵי מִילֵּי הֵיכָא דְּקָא מַגְבַּהּ לֵיהּ אַדַּעְתָּא דְּחַבְרֵיהּ.
phrases.english: These matters are where he is lifting it for him with the knowledge of his friend.

phrases.aramaic: הַאי אַדַּעְתָּא דִּידֵיהּ קָא מַגְבַּהּ לֵיהּ:
phrases.english: This one is lifting it for himself with his own knowledge:
He himself does not acquire,
for others he acquires?!

rashi.hebrew: האי מאי - דקא אמרת הגבהת תופס במוסירה לא הויא הגבהה לדידיה והויא הגבהה לאחריני אפילו אם תימצי לומר כו':
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the Gemara's challenge assumes the principle that lifting an item for another person constitutes acquisition for that person, even if the lifter does not acquire it for themselves.

steps.title: Resolution: Rav Ashi's Explanation
steps.stepSummary: Rav Ashi resolves the challenge by explaining that the rider acquires the donkey and the bridle, while the leader acquires only the part he holds.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב אַשֵּׁי provides a resolution by refining the acquisition details. It is a setup line clarifying the scope of ownership.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The rider acquires the donkey and the bridle. The leader acquires only the part he holds, and the rest remains unacquired by either.

keyTerms.meaning: And the rest was not acquired

phrases.english: Rav Ashi said:
This one acquired the donkey and its halter,
and this one acquired what fits in his hand –
and the remainder he did not acquire,
not this one and not that one.

rashi.hebrew: ובית פגיה - קיבציי"ל בלע"ז מה שבראש החמור וכחמור דמי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that בית פגיה refers to the bridle (קבציי

rashi.hebrew: מה שתפוס בידו - דמה שבתוך ידו הוי מגביה לגמרי:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that מה שתפוס בידו means the leader acquires the bridle because holding it constitutes full lifting (הגבהה) for acquisition purposes.

rashi.hebrew: לא זה ולא זה - והבא לחוטפו יחטוף:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that לא זה ולא זה means neither party has acquired the remainder of the donkey, so whoever grabs it first acquires it.

tosafot.hebrew: והשאר לא קנה לא זה ולא זה - ואפילו אי אמרינן משוך בהמה וקני כלים שעליה קני הכא כיון דתפיס במוסירה לא קני רכוב נמי לא קני כדאמרינן אדעתא דידיה אגבהה איהו לא קני כו':
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that even if one holds that dragging an animal acquires the items on it, here the rider does not acquire the bridle because the leader's holding of it prevents the rider's acquisition, as the leader lifted it for himself.

meforshim.interplaySummary: תוספות addresses a potential objection based on the rule that dragging an animal acquires items on it, explaining why the rider does not acquire the bridle despite this rule, whereas רש״י focuses on defining the terms and the status of the remainder.

steps.title: Rabbi Abbahu's Opinion
steps.stepSummary: Rabbi Abbahu states that the baraita should be understood as written, and the leader acquires because he can detach the reins and bring them to himself.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבִּי אַבָּהוּ offers an alternative explanation for the baraita. It is a setup line introducing a new perspective.
steps.deeperAnalysis: He argues the text should be understood literally. The leader acquires because he has the ability to detach the reins and bring them to himself.

keyTerms.meaning: He can detach it and bring it to himself

phrases.english: Rabbi Abbahu said:
Forever as the small ones –
Since he can detach it and bring it to himself.

rashi.hebrew: כדקתני - זה קנה חמור וזה קנה מוסירה אפילו בית פגיה של חמור:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the phrase כדקתני means both parties acquired something: the leader acquired the donkey and the other party acquired the reins, even if the reins were tied to the donkey's head.

rashi.hebrew: הואיל ויכול - התפוס במוסירה למושכו בכח וינתק מראש החמור ויביאנו אצלו דהואיל וראש החמור גבוה נוח להביאה אצלו בנתיקה אחת:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the leader can acquire the donkey by force because he can detach the reins from the donkey's head and bring them to himself, which is easier since the donkey's head is high.

steps.title: Rejection of Rabbi Abbahu's Opinion
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara rejects Rabbi Abbahu's opinion by arguing that it would lead to an incorrect ruling in a case of a garment half on the ground and half on a pillar.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא rejects רַבִּי אַבָּהוּ's opinion based on a logical inconsistency. It is a setup line demonstrating the flaw.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If his view were correct, it would lead to an incorrect ruling regarding a garment half on the ground and half on a pillar. This forces the rejection of his explanation.

keyTerms.term: טַלִּית שֶׁהִיא מוּנַחַת חֶצְיָהּ עַל גַּבֵּי קַרְקַע
keyTerms.meaning: A garment half lying on the ground

phrases.english: And this statement of Rabbi Abbahu is an error,
For if you do not say so:

phrases.aramaic: טַלִּית שֶׁהִיא מוּנַּחַת חֶצְיָהּ עַל גַּבֵּי
phrases.english: A garment that is lying half on the ground and half on a pillar,
and one came and lifted its half from the ground,

phrases.aramaic: וּבָא אֶחָד וְהִגְבִּיהַּ חֶצְיָהּ מֵעַל גַּבֵּי
phrases.english: and another came and lifted its half from the pillar,

phrases.aramaic: קַרְקַע וּבָא אַחֵר וְהִגְבִּיהַּ חֶצְיָהּ מֵעַל גַּבֵּי עַמּוּד,
phrases.english: Here too, the first one acquires and the latter does not acquire,

phrases.aramaic: הָכִי נָמֵי דְּקַמָּא קָנֵי וּבָתְרָא לָא קָנֵי,
phrases.english: Since he can detach it and bring it to himself?!
Rather, this statement of Rabbi Abbahu is an error.

phrases.aramaic: אֶלָּא הָא דְּרַבִּי אֲבָהוּ בְּרוּתָא הִיא.
phrases.english: Rather, clearly this statement of Rabbi Abbahu is an error.

rashi.hebrew: ברותא היא - דאע"ג דיכול לנתק לאו הגבהה היא:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבי אבהו's opinion is a ברותא because even though one could sever the garment, the act of lifting it does not constitute a valid הגבהה for acquisition.

rashi.hebrew: חציה על גב העמוד - דכיון דבמקום גבוה הוא האוחזה בראשה השני נוח לו לנתק ולהביאה אצלו דלא מחסר הגבהה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that when half the garment is on a pillar, the person holding the other end can easily sever it and bring it to themselves without needing to lift it further, making the acquisition straightforward.

rashi.hebrew: הכי נמי דקמא קני - דמכי אגבהה קניא כולה משום דיכול לנתקה אם כן יש חילוק בשנים שהגביהו מציאה זה מכאן וזה מכאן בין מקום גבוה למקום נמוך דהיכא דשני ראשיה מונחים בקרקע אין הגבהת של ראשון קונה כולה שאין יכול לנתקו ולהביאו אצלו מתוך שהוא ארוך ומונח בקרקע כל מה שהוא נותקו ומושכו אחריו נגרר הוא על גבי קרקע והיכא דאחד מראשיה גבוה כגון בראש החמור או על גבי עמוד המגביה תחלה ראש שעל גבי קרקע קונה כולה ואנן לא אשכחן תנא דמפליג:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י argues that if רבי אבהו's view were correct, the first person to lift the garment from the ground would acquire the entire garment because they could sever it, creating a distinction between lifting from a high place versus a low place that no תנא actually makes.

tosafot.hebrew: דאי לא תימא הכי טלית שחציה כו' - קצת קשה מנ"ל שלא יחלוק רבי אבהו אף בזה:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות questions why the Gemara assumes רבי אבהו would not distinguish in this case of a garment half on the ground and half on a pillar.

tosafot.hebrew: הואיל ויכול לנתקו ולהביאו אצלו - תימה דמשמע דאם נתקה שהוא קונה כו' משום שעל ידו הוגבהה למעלה משלש ומאי שנא מעני המנקף בראש הזית (גיטין דף נט:) שאין בו גזל אלא מפני דרכי שלום וכן פרק הגוזל קמא (ב"ק דף צח. ושם ד"ה והני מילי) הזורק סלע של חבירו לים פטור ומוקי לה בדאדיי' אדויי אלמא לא חשיב מה שהוגבהה על ידו הואיל וסופו ליפול וי"ל דשאני הכא שתופס בידו אחד מן הראש א"נ הכא איירי כשניתק מן העמוד אינו ארוך כ"כ שיגיע ראש השני לארץ ושלהי שילוח הקן (חולין דף קמא:) דקאמר זיל טרוף אקן דלגבהינהו ולקנינהו התם נמי הוגבהו על ידו יותר ממה שהיה תחילה שהרי קצת מפריחים למעלה לכך קני:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות raises a difficulty regarding why severing the garment allows for acquisition when other cases of lifting items that eventually fall do not confer ownership, and answers that holding one end of the garment or severing it from the pillar makes it distinct from those cases.

steps.title: Proof from Rabbi Eliezer
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara cites a baraita of Rabbi Eliezer that states a rider in the field and a leader in the city both acquire.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא brings a proof from a baraita of רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר. It is a setup line introducing a contradictory source.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The baraita states that a rider in the field and a leader in the city both acquire. This suggests riding is a valid method of acquisition, contradicting the previous resolution.

phrases.english: Come and hear, Rabbi Eliezer says:
Sitting on it in the field and leading in the city –
He acquired it! Here too, he leads with his feet.
If so, that is the same as leading!
Two types of leading.

rashi.hebrew: רכוב בשדה - בהמה של מציאה קונה אבל לא בעיר וטעמא מפרש לקמיה ומנהיג אף בעיר קנה וכ"ש בשדה ש"מ רכוב לחודיה קני כו':
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רכוב בשדה acquires an unowned animal (בהמה של מציאה) but not in the city, while מנהיג acquires even in the city, proving that רכוב alone is a valid method of קניין.

steps.title: Challenge: רָכוּב בָּעִיר
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the previous resolution by asking why a rider in the city does not acquire.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא raises a new קושיא against the previous resolution. It argues that if two types of הנהגה exist, then רכיבה in the city should also effect קניין.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The logic is that the prior distinction between types of leading collapses if רכיבה in a city counts. This forces a re-evaluation of the entire framework.

keyTerms.meaning: A logical challenge or difficulty raised against a previous statement.
Riding an animal within city limits.

phrases.aramaic: אִי הָכִי, רָכוּב בָּעִיר מַאי טַעְמָא לָא קָנֵי?
phrases.english: If so, why does one riding in the city not acquire?
Rav Kahana said:

phrases.aramaic: לְפִי שֶׁאֵין דַּרְכָּן שֶׁל בְּנֵי אָדָם לִרְכּוֹב בָּעִיר.
phrases.english: Because it is not the way of people to ride in the city.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִי הָכִי, רָכוּב בָּעִיר מַאי טַעְמָא לָא קָנֵי?

rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the reason people do not typically ride in the city is due to צניעותא, modesty.

tosafot.hebrew: אי הכי רכוב בעיר מאי טעמא לא קני - אי גרסינן א"ה ה"פ אי אמרת בשלמא לא מיירי במנהיג ברגליו לכך בעיר לא קני דלא אזלא מחמתיה לפי שיש בני אדם שמדברים יחד ויש כמה דברים באמצע הדרכים אלא אי אמרינן במנהיג ברגליו א"כ אזלא מחמתיה טובא ואמאי לא קני:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות clarifies the Gemara's challenge by explaining that if the previous case involved leading with one's feet, the animal would move significantly due to the owner's presence, making it unclear why a rider in the city does not acquire.

steps.title: Challenge: קניין on שבת
steps.stepSummary: Rav Ashi challenges Rav Kahana's resolution by arguing that one can acquire on Shabbat even though it is unusual.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב אַשֵׁי raises a fresh קושיא based on רַב כְּהָנָא's resolution. He points out that one can acquire a wallet on שַׁבָּת despite the unusual circumstances.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The challenge is that if קניין requires standard methods, lifting a wallet on שבת should be invalid. Yet the text says מַאי דַּעֲבַד – עֲבַד, implying the acquisition stands.

keyTerms.meaning: The Amora who raises this specific challenge.
The Amora whose resolution is being challenged.
What he did, he did (the action is valid).

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב אָשֵׁי לְרַב כָּהֲנָא:
phrases.english: Rav Ashi said to Rav Kahana:
But now, if he lifted a purse on Shabbat,

phrases.aramaic: שֶׁאֵין דַּרְכָּן שֶׁל בְּנֵי אָדָם לְהַגְבִּיהַּ אַרְנָקִי בְּשַׁבָּת,
phrases.english: since it is not the way of people to lift a purse on Shabbat,
Here too, does he not acquire?
Rather, what he did — he did, and he acquires,

phrases.aramaic: הָכָא נָמֵי מַאי דַּעֲבַד – עֲבַד, וּקְנִי.
phrases.english: Here too, what he did — he did, and he acquires.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֲמַר לֵיהּ רַב אָשֵׁי לְרַב כָּהֲנָא:

steps.title: Resolution: Case of מִקָּח וּמִמְכָּר
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara resolves the challenge by explaining that the baraita is discussing a case of commerce, where the seller told the buyer to acquire in the normal way.
steps.whatsHappening: The גמרא resolves the שבת challenge by specifying the context. It clarifies that the baraita discusses a standard מִקָּח וּמִמְכָּר where the seller instructed the buyer to acquire normally.
steps.deeperAnalysis: This distinction saves the rule that רכיבה alone does not acquire. The acquisition on שבת is valid because it follows the seller's instruction for a standard commercial transfer.

keyTerms.meaning: A resolution or answer to a difficulty.
We are dealing with a case of commerce.
Acquire in the way that people normally acquire.

phrases.aramaic: אֶלָּא בְּמִקָּח וּמִמְכָּר עָסְקִינַן, דַּאֲמַר לֵיהּ:
phrases.english: Rather, we are dealing with a case of purchase and sale, where he said to…
Acquire it the way that two people normally acquire.
